title: [kimi-review] d3c9b8b: [SEVERITY: high] The fix as described appears protocol-incor
description:
Commit: d3c9b8b3a
Message: fix(rpl): validate D-flag in Dao::from_bytes Rejects D=0 per RFC 6550 + SCHC. Closes project-LICHEN-hhgv

Finding: [SEVERITY: high] The fix as described appears protocol-incorrect. Per RFC 6550 §6.4.2, the D flag in a DAO base object simply indicates whether the DODAGID field is present (D=1) or elided (D=0); **both values are valid**. Rejecting D=0 would break legitimate DAO messages, and SCHC compression of RPL explicitly relies on eliding the DODAGID — exactly the D=0 case. If the (missing) code does what the message says, it introduces a correctness bug rather than fixing one. A valid concern would be handling D=0 correctly (i.e., not reading a DODAGID field that isn't there), not rejecting the message.
